Progamming InternThe Programming and Production Department is at the heart of the Segerstrom Center's activities. We curate, produce and partner with our Resident Companies to present over 600 performances and events on multiple stages and public spaces annually. This opportunity offers immersive, hands-on experience in the planning, coordination, budgeting, and execution of a wide range of performances and events. As the Programming intern, you will engage in: Research, budgeting, artist coordination, documentation, archiving, artist support, event scheduling, and logistics. jobs.description: Employer: The House Project Expires: 08/06/2026 We're Hiring: Social Media & Content CoordinatorEntry-Level | Recent Graduate WelcomeReports To: CEO / Clinical DirectorLocation: Laurel, MD — on-site, with occasional community/school site visitsCommitment: Part-time, flexible scheduleCompensation: $500/month stipendAbout the RoleThe House Project (THP) is looking for a creative, curious storyteller to help grow our digital presence. jobs.title: Fall 2026 Internship - Reporters Without Borders - Washington, DC at Reporters Without Borders
jobs.description: Employer: Reporters Without Borders Expires: 08/06/2026 Fall 2026 Internship - Reporters Without Borders USAReporters Without Borders/Reporters sans frontières (RSF), the world’s largest organization devoted to the safety, pluralism, and independence of journalism, is seeking an intern for its Washington, DC office (RSF USA) this fall. About RSF USA RSF USA is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization affiliated with RSF that helps protect press freedom and the right to reliable information in North America, which includes the United States, Canada, and English-speaking Caribbean countries. We also use our position in Washington, DC to work in concert with RSF’s 13 offices around the world to advance our global priorities. Role DescriptionThe internship is ideal for current undergraduate students or recent college graduates who are in the Washington, DC area during the fall and are interested in working in an international nonprofit dedicated to press freedom and the right to reliable information. RSF USA interns contribute to all facets of our work, including advocacy, communications, and fundraising. There may also be flexibility to tailor the intern’s activities to match their background or the practical experience they hope to gain. Specific responsibilities may include but are not limited to: Advocacy:Join frequent advocacy meetings with congressional staff, federal government officials, and NGO partner organizations.Research legislation, court cases, and global press freedom issues.Prepare talking points and Q&A documents on specific issues for meetings with congressional staff.Support urgent RSF actions by working on advocacy strategies across congressional, federal, and state targets.Identify potential partners and champions of RSF USA’s work within the government and learn best practices for building successful coalitions.Build relationships with journalists, lawyers, and NGOs around the country to support the creation of press freedom networks.Represent the RSF DC office in meetings with other RSF offices from around the world.Contribute to advocacy materials like congressional letters and comments to federal agencies.Monitor congressional hearings and help follow up with congressional offices. Communications:Oversee RSF USA’s internal press freedom newsletter by monitoring press freedom issues and sharing updates to RSF’s international staff on a daily basis.Help maintain a list of press contacts and make recommendations for press outreach opportunities. Support the drafting of press releases, social media posts, op-eds, and other written publications.Attend and/or contribute to the planning and execution of press freedom-related events. Fundraising:As needed, minimal assistance with:Building development pipelines through prospect and donor research.Donor record upkeep.Appeals/acquisitions via email and standard mail.Grant applications.Fundraising event support. LogisticsWe will consider applications for both full-time (40 hours per week) and part-time (minimum of 20 hours per week) interns for the fall term, which will start in September and conclude in December. The role operates in a hybrid office environment that requires two in-person work days a week for team collaboration and consistent use of office equipment such as computer, printer, and scanner. Thus, the intern must be residing in the Washington, DC area during the internship. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa for internship opportunities at this time. In addition, the intern is paid a monthly stipend of $600 for part time and $1,000 for full time. Applicant Qualifications:Interested in press freedom, human rights, journalism and/or international nonprofits.Interested in the US policymaking process.Experience communicating with external stakeholders as part of an organization.Excellent communication (written and verbal).Developed research skills, including finding original sources, compiling data in impactful ways, and tailoring outputs for different audiences.Agility in task management, from long-term projects to breaking news responses.An eye for creative solutions to entrenched problems.Familiarity with using various software programs at other organizations.Strong time management.Media literate and an avid news consumer.Able to work in-person in Washington, DC during the summer per our hybrid (in-office/remote) schedule.Detail-oriented.Proactive.Reliable.Native-level English proficiency.French language skills not required, but a plus. Application InstructionsRSF USA welcomes current undergraduate students or recent college graduates that meet the qualifications to apply for this opportunity. We do not recommend that current postgraduate students or recent postgraduates apply. Those interested in applying should submit their 1) resume and 2) cover letter that includes your anticipated weekly availability this fall. To be considered for this opportunity, all applicants must submit their resume and cover letter as a single, combined PDF to hiring@rsf.org by August 5th, 2026 with “RSF Fall 2026 Internship” as the subject line. Reporters Without Borders is an equal opportunity employer and will not discriminate against any applicants for employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, caste, national origin, citizenship, immigration status, veteran status, disability; or on any other basis prohibited by law.

jobs.title: Disney Entertainment Television Production Innovation Intern, Fall 2026 at The Walt Disney Company
jobs.description: Employer: The Walt Disney Company - Disney Entertainment Television Expires: 08/06/2026 About the Role & ProgramDisney Entertainment Television’s Production Innovation team supports the exploration and adoption of emerging technologies across production, post-production, and creative workflows. Partnering with creative, production, and technology teams, the group evaluates new tools and technologies and helps bring scalable solutions into real-world production environments across DET. As a Production Innovation Intern, you will gain hands-on experience supporting Innovation Lab activities, internal projects, and cross-functional initiatives. This is a collaborative, fast-paced environment where you will be exposed to how new technologies are tested, demonstrated, and integrated across a major media organization. This is a full-time internship based in Burbank, CA from approximately August/September 2026 through January 2027. If selected for this role, you will report to Alicia Cota, Manager, Production Innovation. What You Will DoOn any given day, you may:Assist with Innovation Lab tours, demonstrations, and team events, including coordinating logistics, preparing materials, and supporting participant experienceSupport team trackers, databases, and documentation used to monitor initiatives, pilots, and ongoing workstreamsHelp prepare presentation materials, research summaries, and internal communications for creative, production, and technology stakeholdersConduct research on emerging production technologies, tools, and industry trends to support evaluation and experimentationProvide general team support, including maintaining internal resources and assisting with workspace and operational needsParticipate in team meetings, capturing notes and helping track follow-ups and next steps across projects Required Qualifications & SkillsStrong organizational skills and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple prioritiesExcellent written and verbal communication skillsAbility to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environmentProficiency with standard productivity tools (Microsoft Office, Teams, etc.)Interest in television production, media, and emerging technologies Preferred QualificationsRelevant coursework or project experience in media, production, technology, or communicationsFamiliarity with project trackers, databases, or collaboration tools (e.g., Airtable, SharePoint)Hands-on experience with AI tools, creative software, or emerging media technologiesCuriosity about how new technologies are evaluated and applied within the entertainment industry EducationJunior or Senior standing preferredMajor or coursework in Film/Television, Media Studies, Communications, Technology, or a related field preferred Eligibility Requirements & Program InformationCandidates for this opportunity MUST meet all of the below requirements:Be enrolled in an accredited college/university pursuing a degree taking at least one class at time of application OR currently participating in a Disney College Program or Disney Internship.Current Disney Interns, College, International, or Culinary Program Participants: You may only work for ONE consecutive year (12 months) in any combination of an internship and/or program.Be at least 18 years of agePossess unrestricted work authorization Additional InformationAble to have a consistent, reliable work schedule throughout the internshipFully available Monday through Friday for the duration of the internship, approximately 40 hours per weekThis role is based on-site in Burbank, CAAble to provide own housing for the duration of the internship program in the Burbank, CA areaAble to provide reliable transportation to/from work

jobs.title: Digital Intern at Office of Congresswoman Chrissy Houlahan ((PA-06)
jobs.description: Employer: Office of Congresswoman Chrissy Houlahan ((PA-06) Expires: 08/07/2026 Ideal intern applicants are adaptable and proactive self-starters who have experience in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented work environment. Digital interns will be responsible for compiling and distributing press clips, helping develop digital content including graphics and videos, and drafting copy for social media and press materials. Digital interns will also have the opportunity to staff the Congresswoman to capture videos and photos of events. Successful interns in our office are adaptable and proactive self-starters who have experience in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented work environment. Applicants must be independent problem-solvers, have strong writing skills, and be comfortable communicating to both internal and external audiences. An ideal digital intern has technical experience with video editing software (Premiere, Headliner) and/or graphic design tools (Canva, InDesign, Illustrator). Spanish language skills are preferred but not required. Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience, ties to PA-06, and experience with photo editing programs (Photoshop, Lightroom) are all a plus. To apply, please send: 1) a resume, 2) ONE brief paragraph about yourself and why you’re interested in interning in our office, 3) a writing sample, and 4) one example of digital work (video, graphic or photo) to PA06Press.Internships@mail.house.gov.This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.Due to a high volume of applicants, you will likely only be contacted if you are selected for an interview. If you have any questions, please reach out to Nirmal Singletary at nirmal.singletary@mail.house.gov.

jobs.description: Employer: Community Action House Expires: 08/07/2026 Are you looking for meaningful, hands-on experience in nonprofit communications that allows you to build a professional portfolio of writing, marketing, graphic design, and storytelling projects?If so, we'd love for you to join Community Action House as our Communications Intern.The Communications Intern will support key storytelling and marketing efforts that help advance Community Action House’s mission and connect with our broader community. The Communications Intern will assist in monthly communications to program participants, support on tracking campaign success, and support the creation of on-brand graphics and campaign materials. Some college preferred.No supervisory experience requiredBilingual Spanish proficiency preferredPosition DetailsPosition Type: Part-time Internship, 10-15 hours per weekOvertime Status: non-exemptReports To: Communications ManagerDirect Reports: noneDepartment: CommunicationsPrimary Location: Food Club & Opportunity HubPosition Term: September 2026 - May 2027Compensation: $13.73 - $15/hourSupervision Received: Specific Direction: Receives detailed work instructions or guidance for performing repetitive tasks. Supervisor monitors progress and provides feedback or correction as needed to ensure that tasks are completed according to expectations.Supervisory Responsibilities: None: Does not have formal people leading (supervisory) responsibilities.Physical RequirementsFrequently work in stationary position Frequently move about work areaOccasionally use hands/fingers to handle or feelOccasionally reach with hands and armsOccasionally ascend/descend (stairs/ladder/etc.)Occasionally bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawlFrequently use eyesight clarity/accuracy or visual perceptionFrequently read and understand written wordContinuously operate computer and general office machinesOccasionally Work outside in variable weather conditionsOccasionally ability to push, pull, and lift up to 30 pounds Other FactorsAccess to consistent and reliable transportationEvening hours occasionally, on a pre-scheduled basisWeekend hours occasionally, on a pre-scheduled basis$13.73 - $15 an hourAbout Community Action HouseSince its founding in 1969, Community Action House has been the focal point of community efforts to provide critical support and pathways out of poverty for our neighbors in need. Our community-powered work takes a client-centric approach that meets people wherever they are at and offers services that enhance ownership and agency. Our work is organized in three interconnected areas: Food Access, Resource Navigation, and Financial Wellness. Welcome, excellence, and collaboration are some of our core values. (www.communityactionhouse.org)In October 2021, we launched our new ‘Food Club & Opportunity Hub’ to expand efficient, impactful healthy food assistance with the on-site integration of other pathway programs. More recently, we announced a partnership with Dwelling Place to help address the affordable housing crisis here in Holland. In July 2023, we publicly launched our expansion of Lakeshore Food Rescue. (www.lakeshorefoodrescue.org)In April 2026, we are launching our second Resale Store in Holland, MI. Our first Resale Store has proven that it can generate consistent, flexible funding that directly supports our work—while also providing practical resources for the people we serve. A second store allows us to scale that success. It’s a high-impact use of community assets: turning donated goods and volunteer engagement into reliable income, reducing our dependence on fluctuating grants and charitable contributions. (https://www.communityactionhouse.org/resale-expansion)Organizational budget: $14M+ (inclusive of $7-8M of in kind support)Agency Size: 80 staff and 3,000+ annual volunteersOur ValuesWho we are. Who we aspire to be. What drives us. What unites us. > Radically Welcoming> Guest-Centered> Lead with Kindness> Curiosity-Driven> Always Improving> Collaborative by Design> Contagious Commitment> One TeamIf you’re excited to collaborate on our mission, grow your skills, and be part of a team that deeply cares, we’d love to hear from you.ApplicationsAn application is required to be submitted through our Jobs page of our website. Interviews are conducted on a rolling basis. The application process includes a phone interview and two onsite interviews. Community Action House will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations for the hiring process. If a reasonable accommodation is needed, please contact accommodations@communityactionhouse.org - thank you.Equal Opportunity EmployerCommunity Action House is committed to creating a diverse work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, medical condition related to pregnancy or childbirth), sexual orientation, and gender identity, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, height, weight, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected under applicable federal, state, or local laws. jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Sales Development Representative at Inner Peak
jobs.description: Employer: Inner Peak Expires: 08/07/2026 This position is ideal for a recent college graduate looking for their first sales role or a college student looking to earn a little extra money and gain valuable experience. We are a lean team trying to keep costs low. This role is compensated solely based on results - you will be paid per meeting you book for our CEO, as well as a percentage of any deal that closes. There is no base salary, and we have no flexibility on this. If you are not comfortable working on commission only, please do not apply. Summary: Inner Peak AI (www.innerpeak.ai) is blending new AI technology with human therapist expertise to deliver truly personalized, affordable mental wellness support 24/7. We are starting by serving high school and college students but eventually intend to be everyone’s mental wellness safety net, triaging users and delivering them to video content, human therapists, or crisis resources. We are a mission-driven company created out of our founders; own lived experience and struggles with mental health, and are still a small (but motivated) team. Peak’s founders met in undergrad at Harvard a decade ago before reconnecting recently over their shared interest in technology and mental health. We have built and launched a mobile application that has two thousand users and is live at several high schools. Initial student usage data is strong, giving us powerful case studies. To raise our next round of fundraising, we must scale this to more schools. We are looking for hungry interns who care about making a difference in peer mental health and who want high-upside potential through our performance-based compensation. This role is great training for anyone interested in going into sales, as you will report to a sales manager with 10+ years of sales experience. If you are successful at this role, over time it could turn into a full account executive role with a base salary. Responsibilities:You will be acting as a lead-generation sales representative for Inner Peak, talking to real potential customersYou will be calling private and charter school principals, deans of students, directors of counseling, and counselorsYou will learn to use Hubspot, the top startup CRMYour primary goal is to generate interest in Inner Peak’s solution so school leaders will want to see a demo of Inner PeakYour success will be measured in number of meetings booked for our CEO Qualifications:Preference for past sales experience - at minimum you must WANT to sellNo requirement for past sales experience (though it could help), but you have to WANT to sellYou must be personable, friendly, and not intimidated by cold callingInterest in / passion for mental health and / or K-12 educationHard-working, with the ability to put your head down and do callsA self-starter looking to bet on yourself You must be able to devote at least 10 hours a week during normal business hours to the role (though preference for candidates who can devote more)

jobs.title: Intern Associate Interiors at KTGY Group, Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: KTGY Group, Inc. Expires: 08/07/2026 KTGYFounded in 1991, KTGY is a design firm focused on architecture, branding, interiors, and planning specializing in residential, retail, hospitality, and mixed-use environments. Our firm and our work are guided by a continuous focus on innovation, creativity, collaboration, and a deep responsibility we feel towards enhancing communities and neighborhoods. Our architects, designers, and planners combine big-picture opportunities, leading-edge sustainable practices, and impeccable design standards to create memorable destinations of enduring value.Job Title: Intern Associate, Interiors (Fall 2026)Role OverviewAs an Intern Associate, Interiors, you will support the interior design team by assisting with and contributing to active design projects. The ideal candidate is part of a student co-op rotational program and able to work either the spring, summer or fall quarter. You must have a passion for interior design, a positive attitude and unafraid to ask questions. This role is an opportunity to learn and show your talent and initiative. You will work cooperatively with a team lead to support designers, library work, and vendor management. What You Can Expect this FallCollaborate and suggest solutions to solve interior design challenges creatively. Exposure to preparation of plans and elevations during multiple phases of design. Opportunity to assist with the selection of furniture, fixtures and equipment (FFE) including materials and finishes. May have opportunities to work with designers on projects under design and installation.Organize and maintain product library and material samples.Coordinate vendor visits, set up, and clean up.Develop relationships and build camaraderie with your peers, senior designers and leaders. Required Skills and AbilitiesAccurate and detail-oriented.Ability to multi-task and maintain effective communication.Research materials, and color palettes, and prepare palette boards for presentations.Other duties as assigned. Required Experience and Education Minimum of 2 years of college completed.Must be currently enrolled in an accredited Bachelor's or Master's level interior design program KTGY Perks - We Care About Your Health & WellbeingDesign What Works For YouFlexible Alternative Work Week optionsLet’s Partner on Your FutureJump-start your retirement with our 401k matching program - you're 100% vested from day oneA Collaborative Work Environment with FlexibilitySick time and holidays to recharge and celebrateThrive TogetherYour mental health matters: Free unlimited confidential counseling through our EAPStay energized with wellness programs and resourcesThe wage range that the Company expects to pay is $20.00 - $22.00 per hour.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Graphic Design Intern - Public Health at JBS International, Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: JBS International, Inc. Expires: 08/07/2026 JBS’s paid graphic design internship, extending through August 31, 2026, offers an opportunity for graduate and undergraduate students, as well as recent graduates, to gain experience in a range of graphic design tasks and responsibilities. Internship may be part- or full-time depending on selected applicant’s availability. The intern who participates in this program will develop essential skills that are not attained in the academic world and enable them to make the transition from academic to professional life. The Graphic Design intern works collaboratively with the graphic design team to design and create files for print and online materials such as reports, social media designs, brochures, infographics, and promotional campaign materials. The Graphic Design intern will have opportunities to apply and augment their graphic design skills and expertise in a range of areas depending on project needs; areas may include visual style and branding, management of illustrated and photography assets, document formatting and page design, data visualization, copy layout, creation of original and adapted graphic elements for a range of resources, and other aspects of graphic design. The Graphic Design intern will collaborate with senior graphic designers, multimedia specialists (e.g., motion graphics specialists, videographers, instructional designers), and other communications professionals (e.g., writer/editors, technical assistance professionals, communications specialists) at JBS to gain an understanding of client brand standards and to refine the processes used to create visually engaging concepts. The Graphic Design intern will gain experience in and a greater understanding of the federal contracting industry and may help support JBS business development and proposal processes. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS: Assist with designs for a variety of print and online materials which may include reports, PDF tip sheets, presentation slide decks, brochures, promotional campaign materials, social media assets, etc. Understands, maintains, and accurately applies various client brand standards Meets assignment deadlines and maintains process flow May search for stock images and helps maintain stock image asset librariesManages and maintains company-standard file structures for working assets Work with the senior designers to determine best possible design approach to assigned job requestsAssists Graphic Designers in making additions and revisions to existing filesTakes constructive criticism on designs; incorporates revisions from senior graphic designers MINIMUM JOB QUALIFICATIONS:Education: Junior or Senior college student pursuing a Bachelor’s degree OR graduate student pursuing a Master’s degree in graphic design or a related field. Language: English Fluency (Written and Oral) Experience: No direct work experience in graphic design is necessary, but applicants who can demonstrate relevant work experience are preferred. Portfolio (please share link in cover letter or application submission) is necessary and should showcase samples of applicant’s design work from academic and/or professional experiences. Must be able to organize, track and complete multiple tasks during the workday as priorities change. Key qualifications include initiative, organizational skills, ability to work well in a team, and strong communication skills (oral and written). High level of attention to detail.Experience with print and digital media layout Experience using Adobe Creative Cloud programs: InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, Acrobat DCExperience using the standard suite of Microsoft Office products (e.g., Word, PowerPoint, Outlook) Technical Knowledge: Working knowledge of Adobe InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, Acrobat, Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Excel Formatting skills in Word and PowerPoint are highly desirable but not required Basic understanding of Section 508 compliance and accessibility standards is desirable but not required. Competencies: Ability to take direction well and work independently on projects Good communication skills: phone, verbal, and written Ability to interact and collaborate with coworkers and management Strong attention to detail and organization Ability to work quickly and accurately in a fast-paced environment Desire to learn and grow in design skills and software knowledge to keep abreast of industry concepts and developments Location: Remote with the consideration that if a candidate lives within a 50-mile radius of our North Bethesda or San Mateo offices, employment will be considered hybrid. Physical Requirements: Extended Computer Use: Ability to sit for extended periods and operate a computer keyboard and mouse Vision: Must be able to see and read computer screens and printed materials Dexterity: Capable of using hands and fingers to operate equipment and perform tasks requiring precision Communication: Able to effectively communicate verbally and in writing with team members and stakeholders Mobility: Frequently required to stand, walk, reach, and bend to access office equipment and attend meetings Lifting: Ability to lift and carry up to 50 pounds for short distances Sensory Abilities: Must be able to perceive and interact with the environment using hearing and speaking skills Work Environment: Primarily works in a typical office environment, with exposure to computer screens and moderate noise levels OTHER DUTIES AS ASSIGNED: This position description should not be construed to imply that these requirements are the exclusive standards of the position, nor will it be the sole basis for any subsequent employee evaluations. Incumbents will follow any other instructions and perform any other related duties as may be required by their supervisor. This position is subject to availability of funds and to any and all restrictions contained in the contract or contracts that provide funding for this position. APPLICATION INFORMATION:If you meet the minimum requirements for this position, please click on the "Apply" link posted below and complete the application. Please include cover letter, resume, and at least (3) professional references.Our company is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. Applicants can learn more about the company's status as an equal opportunity employer by viewing the federal "EEO" poster at EEOPost.pdf. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or protected Veteran status.If you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please contact us by email at jobs@jbsinternational.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

jobs.title: Social Media Intern at Minnesota Blue Ox
jobs.description: Employer: Minnesota Blue Ox Expires: 08/08/2026 Minnesota Blue Ox Social Media Intern Join Ox Nation. Tell the Story. Build the Brand. The Minnesota Blue Ox Junior Hockey Club is seeking a creative, motivated, and hockey-minded Social Media Intern to help grow the Blue Ox brand while gaining hands-on experience in sports marketing, content creation, and fan engagement. This is an opportunity to work directly with a Tier II NCDC junior hockey organization and create content seen by players, fans, sponsors, and hockey families across North America. Position Overview The Social Media Intern will play a key role in telling the story of the Minnesota Blue Ox through engaging content, gameday coverage, player features, sponsor recognition, and fan interaction.The ideal candidate is creative, self-motivated, and excited to create content using modern social media, video, design, and AI-powered tools. ResponsibilitiesThe Social Media Intern will be responsible for creating and publishing 10–15 pieces of content per week, including: Gameday announcementsGame recaps and final scoresPlayer signing and commitment announcementsPlayer interviews and feature storiesSponsor and community partner highlightsFan engagement posts and interactive contentTeam news and organizational updates Additional responsibilities include:Creating and editing video reels for Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and other platformsCapturing gameday photos and behind-the-scenes contentAssisting with photography at games, practices, camps, and community eventsManaging live gameday social media coverageHelping grow engagement and reach across all social media platformsAssisting with graphic design and content planningUtilizing AI-powered creative tools to assist with graphic design, video editing, captions, content ideas, and fan engagement initiativesWorking with coaches, staff, players, and sponsors to create compelling content that showcases the Blue Ox experience What We’re Looking ForPassion for sports, hockey, marketing, or content creationExperience with Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, and other social media platformsExperience creating content using modern design tools such as Canva, Adobe products, AI-assisted design platforms, and video editing softwareStrong communication and storytelling skillsAbility to work independently and meet deadlinesCreative thinker who is willing to try new ideasStrong organizational skills and attention to detailPhotography and videography experience is a plus but not requiredWhat You’ll GainReal-world sports marketing experiencePortfolio-building content opportunitiesAccess to coaches, players, and team operationsExperience creating content for a Tier II NCDC junior hockey organizationNetworking opportunities throughout the hockey communityHands-on experience using modern sports marketing and AI-powered content creation toolsThe opportunity to help shape and grow one of the Midwest’s fastest-growing junior hockey organizations Compensation This is a paid internship position. Compensation will be discussed based on experience, availability, and scope of responsibilities. Interested? Send a brief introduction along with any examples of your work (social media accounts, videos, graphics, photography, or other content) and tell us why you’d be a great addition to the Minnesota Blue Ox. Help us tell the story of Ox Nation. 🐂💙 Community – TEAM – Championships
